hints.text: Coverage = mainly **Gen3 trekker** in **Punjab** (major temples & tourist sites; very limited)
**Urdu** script (Arabic-like but softer/curvier fonts); **English** also common on signage
Short plates, region-specific — coverage is all Punjab, so mostly a **green strip on the left** (or fully green); India uses long plates
Much coverage in **Mughal gardens** — large ordered gardens w/ stone walls + old forts/temples
Brightly coloured **baggy pants** are common (India's Gen3-trekker clothing tends to be tighter)
**Lahore Fort** (Alamgiri Gate, tall red-brick walls); universities (gothic, colonial white domes, red-brick paths)
Other sites: **Hiran Minar** (temple on a lake), **Rohtas Fort** (near-black walls), **Harappa Museum**, Patriata pine forests
